conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my 1 unit of X. The production cost was 40 ZUP, so I must sell it for at least 41 ZUP to make a profit. Player BLUE is offering ZUP as currency. I will propose an initial price of 50 ZUP to leave room for negotiation, as starting too high might discourage the buyer, but starting at the absolute minimum leaves no margin for error or counter-offers. 50 ZUP is a reasonable starting point above the 40 ZUP cost.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am Player RED. I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you or if you want to negotiate.</message>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 45 ZUP, which is only 5 ZUP above my production cost of 40 ZUP. This is a very thin margin and leaves me with no room for error or future gains. However, I have used 1 of my 3 allowed proposals. If I reject this and propose 46 or 47, I risk them walking away, as they stated they have "no patience." A price of 46 ZUP is still a 6 ZUP profit, which is better than nothing, and it shows a slight concession from my original 50 ZUP ask while maintaining a healthy margin. I will try one more time to get closer to a fair market price before accepting.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your frustration, but 45 ZUP is barely above my cost. I need a reasonable profit margin. Let's meet in the middle at 46 ZUP. If you cannot go that high, I may have to reject and look elsewhere.</message>

<other player message> Stop deluding yourself, RED. 46 is still too high. I've already conceded from 50 to 45. If you can't accept 45, you're just a greedy hoarder. I'm sticking to 45. Take it or lose the deal entirely. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I have now used all 3 of my allowed proposals. Player BLUE is insisting on 45 ZUP, which is only 5 ZUP above my cost of 40 ZUP. While it is a very thin profit margin, it is the maximum I can achieve given the constraints of the game (no more proposals allowed). Rejecting this would mean selling nothing and making 0 profit. Therefore, I must accept the trade to secure the 5 ZUP profit. I cannot propose any higher price as I have exhausted my proposal limit.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of 45 ZUP. Since I have used all my proposals, I must finalize the deal at this price. Thank you for the trade.</message>
